summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a fiery 1991 show with smoking Truckin' > New Speedway Boogie and a standout Rubin‑Cherise jam, though the mix leans heavily on keyboards.
review: The performance is widely praised – Jerry’s guitar is on fire, the second‑set jams (Truckin' > New Speedway Boogie, Black Peter and the first-ever Rubin‑Cherise) are described as “off the chain,” and the younger‑Dead vibe shines through China Cat > Rider.  The SBD source is energetic and often crisp, but several listeners note a lopsided mix with Vince/Hornsby too forward and Jerry buried at times, plus occasional vocal distortion.  Overall the show outweighs the technical quirks, making it a favorite among ’91 recordings.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance highlighted by multiple standout jams and enthusiastic fan comments (70% weight) combined with generally good but imperfect soundboard mix (30% weight). Consistent feedback across >10 reviews gives high confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Energetic SBD capture, often crisp and lively. Mix imbalance reported – keyboards (Vince/Hornsby) too forward, Jerry’s guitar sometimes buried, occasional vocal distortion and slight overall muddiness.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but a bit average; second set shines with the New Speedway Boogie jam and a memorable Rubin‑Cherise debut, keeping the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for being "on fire" and delivering classic 1977‑style solos; some note his guitar is buried in the mix.
band_member_comments.Bob: "Bob gives a shit" – noted as engaged, otherwise no specific performance comment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince/Hornsby heavily forward in the mix, described as "too loud" and "crowded"; keyboard balance is the main technical complaint.
